devanagari: विकालेऽवगाहनाद् वरुणदूतेन वरुणालयं नीतस्य नंदस्य भगवता पुनरनयनम् । श्रीशुक उवाच । एकादश्यां निराहारः समभ्यर्च्य जनार्दनम् । स्नातुं नन्...
english_devnagari: vikāle śrīśuka uvāca ekādaśyāṃ nirāhāraḥ samabhyarcya janārdanam snātuṃ nandastu kālindyāṃ dvādaśyāṃ jalamāviśat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
On the Ekādaśī day (the eleventh day of the lunar fortnight, so sacred to devotees of Viṣṇu) Nanda observed complete fast and worshipped Lord Viṣṇu (Janārdana). On the twelfth day, Nanda entered the waters of the Yamunā for performing ablution.

devanagari: तं गृहीत्वानयद् भृत्यो वरुणस्यासुरोऽन्तिकम् । अवज्ञायासुरीं वेलां प्रविष्टमुदकं निशि ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ gṛhītvānayad bhṛtyo varuṇasyāsuro avajñāyāsurīṃ velāṃ praviṣṭamudakaṃ niśi
translation: Nanda, being ignorant of the time reserved for the demons, entered the waters during night time. A demon (asura) servant of Varuṇa seized Nanda and produced him before Varuṇa.

devanagari: चुक्रुशुस्तमपश्यन्तः कृष्ण रामेति गोपकाः । भगवांस्तदुपश्रुत्य पितरं वरुणाहृतम् । तदन्तिकं गतो राजन् स्वानामभयदो विभुः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: cukruśustamapaśyantaḥ kṛṣṇa rāmeti gopakāḥ bhagavāṃstadupaśrutya pitaraṃ varuṇāhṛtam tadantikaṃ gato rājan svānāmabhayado vibhuḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: Not seeing Nanda, the cowherds cried out, “Oh Kṛṣṇa, Oh Balarāma”. Hearing the cry and knowing that his father had been carried off to Varuṇa, the Omnipresent Lord, who affords protection to his devotees, approached Varuṇa.

devanagari: प्राप्तं वीक्ष्य हृषीकेशं लोकपालः सपर्यया । महत्या पूजयित्वाऽह तद्दर्शनमहोत्सवः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: prāptaṃ vīkṣya hṛṣīkeśaṃ lokapālaḥ saparyayā mahatyā pūjayitvā
translation: Seeing that Lord Kṛṣṇa (the Ruler of senses) had arrived, Varuṇa, the protector of the world, was highly enraptured and worshipping him respectfully submitted:

devanagari: श्रीवरुण उवाच । अद्य मे निभृतो देहो अद्यैवार्थोऽधिगतः प्रभो । त्वत्पादभाजो भगवन् अवापुः पारमध्वनः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīvaruṇa uvāca adya me nibhṛto deho adyaivārtho tvatpādabhājo bhagavan avāpuḥ pāramadhvanaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[imperfect active third plural], [aorist active third plural]

translation:
Varuṇa said:
“Today (the object of assuming) my body has been fulfilled. Though I am the Lord of ocean, which is a mine of precious stones, never before such a treasure-trove was found by me as today.
[Bhāgavata Candrikā. adds:]
By the very sight of you, Oh omnipresent Lord, the (purpose of my) eyes and tongue have become fulfilled.] Those who resort to your feet achieve the end of their journey (of saṃsāra).

devanagari: नमस्तुभ्यं भगवते ब्रह्मणे परमात्मने । न यत्र श्रूयते माया लोकसृष्टिविकल्पना ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: namastubhyaṃ bhagavate brahmaṇe paramātmane na yatra śrūyate māyā lokasṛṣṭivikalpanā
translation: I salute you Oh glorious Lord, the Brahman himself and the Supreme Soul wherein the very name of the Māyā, the cause of creation of the various worlds, is not even heard of.

devanagari: अजानता मामकेन मूढेनाकार्यवेदिना । आनीतोऽयं तव पिता तद् भवान् क्षन्तुमर्हति ॥ ७ ॥
translation: Your father has been brought here by my ignorant servant who did not know the limits and nature of his duty. It behoves your Lordship to pardon him.

devanagari: ममाप्यनुग्रहं कृष्ण कर्तुं अर्हस्यशेषदृक् । गोविन्द नीयतामेष पिता ते पितृवत्सल ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: mamāpyanugrahaṃ kṛṣṇa kartuṃ arhasyaśeṣadṛk govinda nīyatāmeṣa pitā te pitṛvatsala

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active second single], [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Oh Kṛṣṇa! The witness of everything in the Universe! Be pleased to shower your grace on me. Oh Govinda, full of filial love! This is your father. He may be taken back.”
[Bhāgavata Candrikā. adds:]
We have been fortunate to see you as your father has been brought here. We are your progeny (or subjects). It behoves you to be gracious unto us, despite our offences.]”

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । एवं प्रसादितः कृष्णो भगवान् ईश्वरेश्वरः । आदायागात् स्वपितरं बन्धूनां चावहन् मुदम् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca evaṃ prasāditaḥ kṛṣṇo bhagavān īśvareśvaraḥ ādāyāgāt svapitaraṃ bandhūnāṃ cāvahan mudam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[imperfect active third single], [aorist active third single]
[imperfect active second single], [imperfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Thus propitiated by Varuṇa, the glorious Lord Kṛṣṇa, the Supreme Ruler of rulers, took his father with him and returned, thereby bringing delight to his kith and kin.

devanagari: नन्दस्त्वतीन्द्रियं दृष्ट्वा लोकपालमहोदयम् । कृष्णे च सन्नतिं तेषां ज्ञातिभ्यो विस्मितोऽब्रवीत् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: nandastvatīndriyaṃ dṛṣṭvā lokapālamahodayam kṛṣṇe ca sannatiṃ teṣāṃ jñātibhyo vismito
translation: Nanda was struck with wonder at the affluence and grandeur of the protectors of the world which he had never witnessed so far, and also the submissiveness of the Lokapālas to Kṛṣṇa—with amazement he related everything to his relatives.

devanagari: ते चौत्सुक्यधियो राजन् मत्वा गोपास्तमीश्वरम् । अपि नः स्वगतिं सूक्ष्मां उपाधास्यदधीश्वरः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: te cautsukyadhiyo rājan matvā gopāstamīśvaram api naḥ svagatiṃ sūkṣmāṃ upādhāsyadadhīśvaraḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: Thinking him to be the Almighty Lord himself, the cowherds whose minds were still more overwhelmed with eagerness and wonder, thought (to themselves): “Would that the Supreme Lord grant unto us his imperceptible state (called Brahman) and take us to his eternal region?”

devanagari: इति स्वानां स भगवान् विज्ञायाखिलदृक् स्वयम् । सङ्कल्पसिद्धये तेषां कृपयैतदचिन्तयत् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: iti svānāṃ sa bhagavān vijñāyākhiladṛk svayam saṅkalpasiddhaye teṣāṃ kṛpayaitadacintayat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active second single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: Knowing the wish (cherished at heart) by his own people, the all-perceiving glorious Lord, out of his own accord, and out of grace, began to think with a view to accomplishing their desires.

devanagari: जनो वै लोक एतस्मिन् अविद्याकामकर्मभिः । उच्चावचासु गतिषु न वेद स्वां गतिं भ्रमन् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: jano vai loka etasmin avidyākāmakarmabhiḥ uccāvacāsu gatiṣu na veda svāṃ gatiṃ bhraman

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: “While wandering in this world in higher and lower forms of existence due to the force of the Nescience, desire and actions (karma), people verily do not know their own fate.”

devanagari: इति सञ्चिन्त्य भगवान् महाकारुणिको हरिः । दर्शयामास लोकं स्वं गोपानां तमसः परम् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: iti sañcintya bhagavān mahākāruṇiko hariḥ darśayāmāsa lokaṃ svaṃ gopānāṃ tamasaḥ param
translation: Considering this, the highly compassionate, glorious Lord Hari manifested to the cowherds his own realm which is beyond the range of tamas and his essential nature which is absolutely real, not subject to modification or destruction but is pure consciousness, infinite, self-luminous, eternal and perfect stage which is realized by the sages after transcending the trinity of guṇas (when the bondage of guṇas is severed).

devanagari: सत्यं ज्ञानमनन्तं यद् ब्रह्म ज्योतिः सनातनम् । यद्धि पश्यन्ति मुनयो गुणापाये समाहिताः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: satyaṃ jñānamanantaṃ yad brahma jyotiḥ sanātanam yaddhi paśyanti munayo guṇāpāye samāhitāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Considering this, the highly compassionate, glorious Lord Hari manifested to the cowherds his own realm which is beyond the range of tamas and his essential nature which is absolutely real, not subject to modification or destruction but is pure consciousness, infinite, self-luminous, eternal and perfect stage which is realized by the sages after transcending the trinity of guṇas (when the bondage of guṇas is severed).

devanagari: ते तु ब्रह्मह्रदं नीता मग्नाः कृष्णेन चोद्धृताः । ददृशुर्ब्रह्मणो लोकं यत्राक्रूरोऽध्यगात् पुरा ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: te tu brahmahradaṃ nītā magnāḥ kṛṣṇena coddhṛtāḥ dadṛśurbrahmaṇo lokaṃ yatrākrūro

[nominative plural from √ majj class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ majj class 6 verb], [accusative plural from √ majj class 6 verb]
[imperfect active third single], [aorist active third single]

translation: They were at first transported to Brahmahrada[1]—pool-like deeply absorbed state in Brahman—and were made to plunge into it and were eventually taken out of it (i.e. were awakened from that samādhi or trance). There they beheld the divine region of Brahman by the Grace of Lord Kṛṣṇa through which formerly[2] Akrūra also saw the divine abode.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
SR.’s 1st explanation: Para-Brahman is the pool in which the cowherds were made to remain immersed in a trance and again roused from the samādhi by Kṛṣṇa.
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā’s 2nd explanation: The pool in the Yamunā wherein the Akrūra beheld the Brahma-loka (vide infra chs. 39.40-55) is also called Brahma-hrada. Although Bhāvāratha Dīpikā gives this alternative explanation that they were taken to this pool in the Yamunā by Kṛṣṇa and were shown Brahma-loka that is not complementary to the Almighty Lord Kṛṣṇa. Hence Bhāvāratha Dīpikā’s first explanation is accepted.

Footnote [2]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā explains: “The word purā is used as the incident took place before the dialogue between Śuka and Parīkṣit took place.”

devanagari: नन्दादयस्तु तं दृष्ट्वा परमानन्दनिवृताः । कृष्णं च तत्रच्छन्दोभिः स्तूयमानं सुविस्मिताः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: nandādayastu taṃ dṛṣṭvā paramānandanivṛtāḥ kṛṣṇaṃ ca tatracchandobhiḥ stūyamānaṃ suvismitāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ stu class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ stu class 2 verb]

translation:
Beholding in that realm Kṛṣṇa being extolled by personified Vedas in different hymns, Nanda and others were filled with supremely ecstatic delight and were greatly amazed as well.
[back to top]

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्‍भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां । संहितायां दशमस्कन्धे पूर्वार्धे अष्टाविंशोऽध्यायः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmad‍bhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃ saṃhitāyāṃ daśamaskandhe pūrvārdhe aṣṭāviṃśo
